ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general duck food product class (FOODON_03302051); no specific roasted duck with truffle sauce term exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cooked duck meat broadly; no specific truffle-dressed roasted duck code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept found for roasted duck with truffle sauce. SNOMED CT food hierarchy does not include this preparation.

serving.description: 1 portion (half duck or quarter duck with sauce)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of roasted duck with truffle sauce is approximately 300 g, representing a quarter to half duck portion dressed with truffle sauce, suitable for one adult as a main course.

nutrient_source.title: Duck, domesticated, meat and skin, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05141)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy entry for roasted duck (meat and skin). Truffle sauce contributes minimal macronutrient change at typical dressing quantities (~10–15 g per 100 g serving); sodium adjusted upward to reflect soy-based truffle sauce seasoning typical of Chinese restaurant preparation. Values represent a composite estimate of roasted duck with truffle sauce as served.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: Roasted duck with truffle sauce has no direct GI measurement in the University of Sydney GI Database. Duck is a high-protein, high-fat, very low-carbohydrate food (carbohydrate_g ~1.5 per 100 g). By analogy with other high-protein, low-carbohydrate meat dishes (GI < 15 per published tables), GI is estimated at 15, placing it firmly in the low category (≤ 55). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (15 × 4.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 0.7, confirming negligible glycemic impact.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy-based truffle sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its elevated sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g), which can contribute substantially to daily sodium limits when consumed in a typical 300 g restaurant portion (~1560 mg sodium per serving).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content and negligible glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Roasted duck with truffle sauce has a very low carbohydrate content (~1.5 g per 100 g) and an estimated glycemic index of 15, making it a low-glycemic-load food suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions; however, the high saturated fat content warrants attention in the context of overall dietary fat management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat and saturated fat with elevated sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high total fat (~28 g per 100 g) and saturated fat (~9.5 g per 100 g) content, combined with elevated sodium from the truffle sauce, make this dish a moderate cardiovascular concern; individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume it occasionally and in small portions as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Roasted Duck with Truffle (松露烤鸭) is a premium Chinese restaurant dish featuring whole duck that has been marinated in a blend of soy sauce, five-spice powder, garlic, and ginger, then roasted at high heat until the skin is lacquered and crisp. The duck is finished with a rich truffle sauce — typically made from black or white truffle, truffle oil, and a soy-based reduction — which imparts an earthy, umami-forward flavour profile that elevates the classic Cantonese roast duck preparation. The flesh is tender and succulent with a pronounced savoury depth, while the skin retains its characteristic crispness. In Singapore, this dish is served in upscale Chinese restaurants and dim sum establishments as a premium main course, often priced at SGD 60 or above for a whole or half duck. Nutritionally, roasted duck with truffle sauce is energy-dense (~337 kcal per 100 g), high in protein (~19 g) and fat (~28 g, of which ~9.5 g is saturated), and very low in carbohydrates (~1.5 g), resulting in a negligible glycemic load. The sodium content is elevated (~520 mg per 100 g) due to the soy-based sauce, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish provides meaningful amounts of iron, zinc, B vitamins, and phosphorus from the duck meat.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for roasted duck with truffle sauce exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy roasted duck (meat and skin, NDB 05141) with sodium adjusted upward to reflect soy-based truffle sauce seasoning. GI is estimated by analogy with low-carbohydrate meat dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy (roasted duck, meat and skin) with sodium increased to reflect soy-based truffle sauce contribution typical of Chinese restaurant preparation. An exact HPB or ICMR entry for this specific dish was not found. 2. Vitamin C is 0.0 mg per 100 g; this is confirmed zero for roasted duck meat and skin per USDA FoodData Central — confirmed zero per approved source. 3. Glycemic index is set to 15 (estimated) based on analogy with high-protein, very low-carbohydrate meat dishes; no direct GI measurement exists for this dish. GI category is low (15 ≤ 55). Glycemic load per 300 g serving = 0.7. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are populated from USDA SR Legacy roasted duck data and are not sentinel values. 5. Halal status is marked false as duck preparation with truffle sauce in Chinese restaurants may involve non-halal soy sauces or cooking methods; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 6. Price SGD 60.0 per dish as reported by field survey data (Loong Dim Sum, North Canal Road) — preserved from input record for reference only; not used as a nutritional source.
